title: The One with the Fake Party

jokes.text: Cereal killer. / Cereal killer? / Hey, come on, I say more dumb things before 9 a.m... than most people say all day.
jokes.setup: Character mishears or mispronounces 'serial killer' as 'cereal killer'
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission about saying dumb things early in the morning
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay with self-aware follow-up that shows character awareness of their own flaws. Economical structure.

jokes.text: I'm telling you, being pregnant is no piece of cake. Oh, cake!
jokes.setup: Uses idiom 'no piece of cake' to describe pregnancy difficulty
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets distracted by the actual word 'cake' due to pregnancy cravings
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Phoebe - the distraction by literal cake after using the idiom is very much her voice. Excellent economy and structure.

jokes.text: It's me. It's Phoebe. Listen, there's something in here I want to eat.
jokes.setup: Phoebe calling from the bathroom about wanting to eat something
jokes.punchline: The absurd location and phrasing creates uncomfortable comedy
jokes.explanation: Absurd Phoebe moment that plays on pregnancy cravings in an unexpected location. Solid character work.

jokes.text: Yes. Yes. Yes. I can't believe it. The baby wants bologna. The baby wants meat. I can't eat meat.
jokes.setup: Phoebe discovers her pregnancy craving is for bologna
jokes.punchline: Vegetarian Phoebe's moral conflict with her baby's meat cravings
jokes.explanation: Great character conflict setup for Phoebe's vegetarian beliefs vs pregnancy cravings. Sets up ongoing storyline.

jokes.text: Joshua, give me a call sometime. Guys like you... never go out of style.
jokes.setup: Rachel writing a note to put in a coat she's selling to Joshua
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun combining 'style' with coat/fashion context
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad pun that's meant to be cringey. Works for Rachel's desperation but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: We went to see a collection of Victorian doorknobs at the museum. / Without me?
jokes.setup: Ross and Emily went to see Victorian doorknobs
jokes.punchline: Someone's mock outrage at being excluded from the boring museum exhibit
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about being left out of something incredibly boring. Clean structure.

jokes.text: I don't know how museums work in England, but here, you're not supposed to take stuff.
jokes.setup: Emily shows off a Victorian doorknob from the museum
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation about museum theft rules
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm with perfect timing and delivery. Very much his voice and economical.

jokes.text: I got it from the gift shop. / They have really lax security there.
jokes.setup: Emily explains she got the doorknob from gift shop
jokes.punchline: Chandler doubles down on his theft joke
jokes.explanation: Chandler continuing the bit but it's not as strong as the initial joke. Functional follow-up.

jokes.text: It's a joke. / Anyone for more tea? / No, just me then. / Hey, Ringo.
jokes.setup: Awkward moment after Chandler's joke, Ross offers tea in British manner
jokes.punchline: Chandler calling Ross 'Ringo' for his British affectations
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy about Ross adopting British mannerisms. 'Ringo' is a clever reference.

jokes.text: Every time Emily's around, you talk like her. Would you please cut it out? / I think you like it. / I think you can't resist me when I do it.
jokes.setup: Chandler calls out Ross for adopting Emily's speech patterns
jokes.punchline: Ross suggesting Chandler is attracted to his British accent
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics and Ross's mock-romantic tone adds humor to the situation.

jokes.text: You want to eat me up like a cream 'pumpy.' / What? / All right, look, I don't know all the words.
jokes.setup: Ross attempting to say something British and romantic
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't know the actual British expressions
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ross moment - his attempt at British slang and immediate confession works perfectly for his character. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Right. I've got to be off. I'll see you. Bye-bye, then. / Bye-bye, then.
jokes.setup: Emily uses British farewell
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately copies her exact phrasing
jokes.explanation: Direct callback to the established bit about Ross copying Emily. Works as character consistency.

jokes.text: When I'm with her, I'm fun! I do things like run off to Vermont... and you guys saw the doorknob.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining how Emily makes him more adventurous
jokes.punchline: Citing the doorknob museum as evidence of his wild side
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - Ross's idea of 'fun' being museum visits perfectly captures his personality. Great callback usage.

jokes.text: Hey, that's what all my relationships are like. / Yes, but in Ross' case, they both know in two weeks that's it.
jokes.setup: Joey relates to Ross's two-week relationship
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's observation that Ross's relationships have mutual awareness vs Joey's one-sided
jokes.explanation: Sharp character-based humor that works for both Joey (oblivious) and Phoebe (insightful). Well-structured burn.

jokes.text: Maybe that's because soy burgers suck. / But I'm no obstetrician....
jokes.setup: Phoebe got nauseous from soy burger meant to trick baby
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt assessment followed by mock-medical disclaimer
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment - his straightforward honesty about food followed by the obstetrician line is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: That's sweat. You throw up all morning, you'll have that glow too.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Phoebe's pregnancy glow
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's blunt explanation that it's just sweat from morning sickness
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's characteristically blunt deflation of pregnancy romanticization. Good economy.

jokes.text: It's comfortable. You could really flash somebody in this.
jokes.setup: Joshua trying on a trench coat
jokes.punchline: Innocent comment about the coat's flashing potential
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo that's more awkward than funny. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Why not? / Well, because... we get a lot of... / They ruin it for everybody.
jokes.setup: Joshua asks why he can't put hands in pockets
jokes.punchline: Rachel's incomplete explanation about shoplifters, trailing off awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Awkward beat comedy but not particularly strong. Shows Rachel's nervousness around Joshua.

jokes.text: Congratulations. So do you love her?
jokes.setup: Joshua mentions first date since divorce
jokes.punchline: Rachel's immediate jump to 'do you love her'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Rachel's romantic desperation and poor social timing. Economical.

jokes.text: What was that? / Just an anti-theft device. / Then what's this? / You need that too, because a thief could just tear this up.
jokes.setup: Security tag goes off on Joshua
jokes.punchline: Rachel's increasingly absurd explanations for multiple security devices
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity but feels a bit forced. Shows Rachel's nervousness leading to over-explanation.

jokes.text: That sucks. Nobody's ever thrown me a bon-voyage-Emily party.
jokes.setup: Monica announces they need to throw Emily a bon voyage party
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mock jealousy about never getting his own bon-voyage-Emily party
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler wit - the specificity of 'bon-voyage-Emily party' makes it funnier than generic complaint. Great economy.

jokes.text: As much as I'd like to meet Josh and warn him...
jokes.setup: Rachel explains her plan to seduce Joshua at the party
jokes.punchline: Chandler's protective/warning instinct about Joshua
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler moment showing his protective/cynical nature about Rachel's romantic schemes.

jokes.text: Surprise! / No one's thrown me a surprise party before. / Well, it was all Ross' idea.
jokes.setup: Emily arrives to surprise party supposedly for her
jokes.punchline: Emily's genuine gratitude for a party that's actually for someone else
jokes.explanation: Ironic situation comedy - Emily's genuine appreciation for fake party creates uncomfortable humor.

jokes.text: You're so sweet. / And I'm so surprised.
jokes.setup: Emily continuing to thank them for the surprise party
jokes.punchline: Her genuine surprise at a party not actually meant for her
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the ironic situation but not adding much new comedic value.

jokes.text: You didn't know?
jokes.setup: Emily expressing surprise about the party
jokes.punchline: Ross's confused question revealing the deception
jokes.explanation: Awkward beat that heightens the uncomfortable situation but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Quick! He's looking. Say something funny. / Like what? / What's so funny? / I said, 'Like what?' / Now that's a thinker.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks Monica to say something funny to impress Joshua
jokes.punchline: The meta-joke about 'Like what?' being the funny thing, then Joshua's dry response
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor about the difficulty of being funny on command. Joshua's deadpan response adds to it.

jokes.text: Care for a cherry? / No, thanks. / I can tie one of these into a knot using just my tongue.
jokes.setup: Rachel offering cherries to Joshua
jokes.punchline: Rachel's attempt at seduction through cherry stem trick
jokes.explanation: Classic seduction trope that's more cringe than clever. Works for Rachel's desperation character moment.

jokes.text: You okay? / You all right?
jokes.setup: Rachel presumably chokes or fails at cherry trick
jokes.punchline: Concerned reactions implying Rachel's cherry trick went wrong
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy payoff to the cherry setup. Functional physical comedy but predictable.

jokes.text: Actually, American surprise parties are very short. It's usually, 'Surprise!' Then, 'My God, I'm so surprised. Bye!'
jokes.setup: Ross asks why the party is ending when Emily wants to stay
jokes.punchline: Rachel's fake explanation about American party customs
jokes.explanation: Good improvisational lie from Rachel that sounds plausible while being absurd. Well-structured with good timing.

jokes.text: Your sister told me that you used to dress up like old ladies... and host make-believe tea parties.
jokes.setup: Emily sharing embarrassing Ross story Monica told her
jokes.punchline: Ross's childhood cross-dressing tea party hobby
jokes.explanation: Good embarrassing backstory reveal that fits Ross's character. Sets up his retaliation against Monica.

jokes.text: Did she tell you that when she was little... she was partially responsible for new legislation... regulating the strength of swing sets?
jokes.setup: Ross retaliating against Monica for sharing embarrassing story
jokes.punchline: Monica's childhood accident was so bad it led to safety legislation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and character work. The specificity of 'swing set legislation' is brilliantly absurd yet believable for Monica.

jokes.text: Then what are you going to put on top of that? / A little salami. / Oh, yeah. / What goes on top of the salami? / Pastrami. / You're a genius.
jokes.setup: Phoebe building an elaborate meat sandwich
jokes.punchline: Joey's enthusiasm for the meat-on-meat escalation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for both - Phoebe's meat obsession and Joey's food enthusiasm. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: You changed? / I decided I needed my lucky dress. / Lucky means more cleavage? / It does for me.
jokes.setup: Rachel changes into a different dress
jokes.punchline: Rachel's honest admission that luck equals cleavage for her
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Rachel's straightforward approach to using her assets. Economical and honest.

jokes.text: This scene in Footloose- / Flashdance. / Yeah. With that plumber girl? / A welder.
jokes.setup: Monica suggests the bra removal trick from a movie
jokes.punchline: Their confusion about movie details while discussing the sexy scene
jokes.explanation: Good escalating confusion about pop culture details. Shows their focus on the wrong aspects of the reference.

jokes.text: Were you, like, in the movie?
jokes.setup: Monica knows oddly specific details about the welder
jokes.punchline: Rachel's confusion about Monica's detailed knowledge
jokes.explanation: Nice beat about Monica's know-it-all tendencies. Good economy in the delivery.

jokes.text: Or you could use mistletoe. / It's not Christmas. / Spin-the-bottle. / He's not 11.
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting seduction methods
jokes.punchline: Monica and Chandler's logical objections to each suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good rapid-fire exchange with logical objections. Shows different character approaches to problem-solving.

jokes.text: You can't leave yet. You have to stay. We've got the whole big thing planned. / What big thing? / Spin-the-bottle works like this: I spin the bottle.
jokes.setup: Rachel desperately trying to keep Joshua at party
jokes.punchline: Monica implementing the ridiculous spin-the-bottle plan
jokes.explanation: Escalation of Rachel's desperation and Monica's loyalty. Sets up the cringe spin-the-bottle sequence.

jokes.text: Welcome to America.
jokes.setup: Emily gets kissed in spin-the-bottle
jokes.punchline: Joey's dry comment about American customs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey deadpan delivery with excellent economy. Great timing and very quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Two in a row! You gotta use your tongues now.
jokes.setup: Emily gets chosen twice in spin-the-bottle
jokes.punchline: Monica's made-up escalation rule about tongues
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive nature creating absurd rules. Creates more uncomfortable comedy.

jokes.text: What are the odds? / That's enough!
jokes.setup: Emily gets chosen again in spin-the-bottle
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation and Ross's protective outburst
jokes.explanation: Good character reactions showing Chandler's dry humor and Ross's jealousy. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Story of my life. / Story of my father's life.
jokes.setup: Gunther gets chosen and makes self-deprecating comment
jokes.punchline: Escalates to include his father in the pattern of loneliness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gunther moment - his tragic comic relief escalation is very much his character. Great economy and structure.

jokes.text: The baby just kicked! / It's okay. It's okay. If it kicked once, it'll kick again! / Everybody just remember where they were sitting.
jokes.setup: Phoebe feels baby kick during spin-the-bottle
jokes.punchline: Monica's competitive response treating it like a game and preserving the moment
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work for Monica's competitive nature applied to pregnancy. Great absurd escalation.

jokes.text: It was a bug.
jokes.setup: Everyone waiting for baby to kick again
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual correction that it wasn't the baby at all
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent economy. Classic Phoebe deflation of a moment everyone was invested in.

jokes.text: You know why I'm never gonna eat meat? Because it's cold-blooded murder.
jokes.setup: Phoebe stating her vegetarian principles
jokes.punchline: The irony given she's actively craving and about to eat meat
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction showing Phoebe's internal struggle. Sets up her fall from grace.

jokes.text: There's a Phoebe on my sandwich!
jokes.setup: Someone discovers Phoebe eating their sandwich
jokes.punchline: Play on 'fly in my soup' complaint but with Phoebe as the pest
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on classic restaurant complaint. Good visual comedy and very quotable structure.

jokes.text: You know when you're dating someone... and you don't want to cheat unless it's with someone really hot? / Totally. / This is the same kind of deal.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining her meat-eating as moral compromise
jokes.punchline: Comparing vegetarian cheating to romantic cheating with Joey's enthusiastic agreement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy that perfectly captures Phoebe's logic and Joey's moral flexibility. Very quotable and well-structured.

jokes.text: If you're gonna do something wrong... do it right.
jokes.setup: Phoebe justifying her meat binge
jokes.punchline: Her twisted moral philosophy about committing to your transgressions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe wisdom - sounds profound but is completely backwards. Very quotable and economical.

jokes.text: I'm trying to get Emily out of here, and you keep talking to her... being all interesting and making her laugh. I don't want you to be funny anymore.
jokes.setup: Ross frustrated with Chandler entertaining Emily
jokes.punchline: Ross's request for Chandler to stop being funny is absurd
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's possessiveness and Chandler's natural charm being a problem.

jokes.text: From now on, I'm gonna be funny sometimes, and not funny others.
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel have conflicting demands about Chandler being funny
jokes.punchline: Chandler's compromise to selectively deploy his humor
jokes.explanation: Great meta-humor about Chandler's role as the funny guy. Very much his character to analyze the situation logically.

jokes.text: Six more months, three meals a day. I am going to eat, like, you know, millions of cows. / Mommy cows, daddy cows, baby cows / No. No, I will never eat baby cows. No veal! / Oh, but veal.
jokes.setup: Phoebe calculating how much meat she'll eat during pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Her horrified realization about baby cows then immediate temptation by veal
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Phoebe's moral conflict. The 'Oh, but veal' moment is perfect character work.

jokes.text: What if I even things out for you, meat-wise? / What? / I eat a lot of meat, right? But suppose, until the baby's born, I laid off it. / No extra animals would die. You'd be eating my animals.
jokes.setup: Joey proposing solution to Phoebe's moral dilemma
jokes.punchline: Joey's meat quota theory and 'eating my animals'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey logic that's both absurd and oddly sweet. 'You'd be eating my animals' is iconic. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: There's no meat in beer, right?
jokes.setup: Joey agreeing to be vegetarian for Phoebe
jokes.punchline: His immediate concern about whether beer contains meat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment showing his priorities and limited understanding of vegetarianism. Very economical.

jokes.text: Rachel is going to put on a skit.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining why they can't leave
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of Rachel performing a skit to keep Joshua there
jokes.explanation: Sets up the cringe cheerleader moment. Works as escalation of Rachel's desperation.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Have you lost your mind? / I am finally thinking clearly.
jokes.setup: Monica's horror at seeing Rachel in cheerleader outfit
jokes.punchline: Rachel's confident assertion that this is clear thinking
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between what looks insane and Rachel's confidence. Sets up the cheerleader disaster.

jokes.text: My lucky dress wasn't working out too well for me. But for four years, this baby never missed.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining her cheerleader costume choice
jokes.punchline: Her confidence in the cheerleader outfit's track record
jokes.explanation: Good character logic showing Rachel's past success giving her false confidence for this situation.

jokes.text: Nice costume.
jokes.setup: Rachel appears in cheerleader outfit
jokes.punchline: Joshua's understated reaction to the inappropriate costume
jokes.explanation: Understated reaction that heightens the cringe. Economical but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Give me an 'E'! Give me an 'M'! Give me an 'I'! Give me an 'L'! Give me a 'Y'! What do you get? Emily! Emily!
jokes.setup: Rachel performing cheerleader routine
jokes.punchline: The full cringe of adult Rachel doing high school cheer for Emily
jokes.explanation: Pure cringe comedy that works through Rachel's desperation but isn't particularly clever writing.

jokes.text: That's me as a cheerleader!
jokes.setup: Rachel finishing her cheer routine
jokes.punchline: Her unnecessary explanation of what everyone just witnessed
jokes.explanation: Captain Obvious moment that adds to the cringe but isn't particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: I loosened a tooth. It's no big deal. I have a dentist.
jokes.setup: Rachel injured herself during the cheer
jokes.punchline: Her casual dismissal of loosening a tooth while trying to maintain dignity
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff with Rachel trying to maintain composure after disaster.

jokes.text: What do I do now? / I think you're done. / Time to take off the bra.
jokes.setup: Rachel's cheer routine was a disaster
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt assessment and suggestion to move to the nuclear option
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Monica's practical approach to the disaster and callback to earlier plan.

jokes.text: That was really great. But I gotta take off. / Take the bra off.
jokes.setup: Joshua politely trying to leave
jokes.punchline: Monica's whispered desperate instruction about the bra creates double meaning
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with 'take off' having double meaning. Monica's desperation is palpable.

jokes.text: Rachel's my girlfriend.
jokes.setup: Joshua and Rachel heading to her room
jokes.punchline: Ross's confused/protective interjection
jokes.explanation: Awkward beat showing Ross's confusion but not particularly funny. More plot than comedy.

jokes.text: This used to be my bedroom. A lot of memories in here. Lot of memories.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to seduce Joshua in her childhood bedroom
jokes.punchline: Her awkward attempt at creating romantic atmosphere with childhood memories
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup showing Rachel's poor seduction skills in inappropriate setting.

jokes.text: If these walls could talk, they'd say: 'Want to hear some memories?'
jokes.setup: Rachel continuing her awkward bedroom seduction
jokes.punchline: Her terrible attempt at the 'if walls could talk' cliché
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of the cliché with Rachel's awkward execution. Very much her character under pressure.

jokes.text: God! Forget it!
jokes.setup: Rachel struggling with the bra removal trick
jokes.punchline: Her frustrated abandonment of the seduction technique
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff to the Flashdance setup. Shows Rachel's mounting frustration.

jokes.text: I don't wear suits to work. And I bought six of them from you.
jokes.setup: Joshua explaining why he kept coming back to the store
jokes.punchline: Admission that he has no use for the suits he bought
jokes.explanation: Good revelation showing Joshua's attraction to Rachel. Economical way to show his interest.

jokes.text: But- / No 'but.' 'But' is never good. Let's leave it at 'l like you and you like me.' / Okay. However- / No, that's a fancy 'but.'
jokes.setup: Joshua trying to explain his hesitation about dating
jokes.punchline: Rachel's analysis of 'however' as a fancy 'but'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay showing Rachel's desperation to avoid bad news. 'Fancy but' is very quotable and clever.

jokes.text: If it makes you feel any better, I made a fool of myself. / Helps a little.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to make Ross feel better about his ruined evening
jokes.punchline: Ross's honest admission that her misery helps him
jokes.explanation: Good honest moment showing Ross's petty satisfaction. Economical and in character.

jokes.text: Is there room on that step for a pathetic loser? / Have a seat.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking to join Ross on the step
jokes.punchline: Ross's welcoming response to fellow loser
jokes.explanation: Nice bonding moment over mutual failure. Good character dynamics and economical.

jokes.text: And you're sitting here in a hallway... with a 28-year-old cheerleader with a fat lip.
jokes.setup: Rachel pointing out Ross should go after Emily
jokes.punchline: Her self-deprecating description of herself
jokes.explanation: Great self-awareness and callback to her earlier disasters. Good reality check moment.

jokes.text: What photo album was it? / I don't know. It was you and a bunch of albino kids.
jokes.setup: Ross asking which embarrassing photos Emily saw
jokes.punchline: Rachel's description of what she thought were albino kids
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the computer camp punchline. Rachel's innocent misunderstanding is funny.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Those weren't albino kids. That was computer camp!
jokes.setup: Rachel's description of albino kids in photos
jokes.punchline: Ross's horrified realization about his computer camp photos
jokes.explanation: Excellent punchline that reframes Rachel's description perfectly. Very Ross to be embarrassed by computer camp.

jokes.text: You're a pathetic loser, right? / Oh, yeah. / Sit!
jokes.setup: Monica encountering Joshua after his rejection
jokes.punchline: Her welcoming him to the pathetic loser club with Ross and Rachel
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the pathetic losers theme. Monica's directness is in character.

jokes.text: I love that 'but.'
jokes.setup: Joshua says 'but' after his previous rejection of it
jokes.punchline: Rachel's joy at hearing the word she previously banned
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the earlier 'but' vs 'however' conversation. Shows Rachel's hope.

jokes.text: Every time.
jokes.setup: Rachel and Joshua go into Central Perk together
jokes.punchline: Gunther's resigned observation about losing Rachel to other men
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gunther moment - his perpetual romantic disappointment summed up economically. Very much his character.

jokes.text: What do you got there? / Pastrami. / You know what goes good with that? / Corned beef. / I was going to say bologna. But that's better.
jokes.setup: Joey and Phoebe continuing their meat sandwich building
jokes.punchline: Joey's meat expertise and Phoebe's deference to his knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the meat theme with Joey's expertise. Shows their partnership.

jokes.text: How about that smoked turkey? / Okay. / Oh, mama!
jokes.setup: More sandwich building between Joey and Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Joey's orgasmic reaction to the meat combination
jokes.explanation: Joey's food enthusiasm is in character but this particular moment isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: When is the baby due? / Six months.
jokes.setup: Joey asking about Phoebe's pregnancy timeline
jokes.punchline: The implication that they have six months of this meat obsession
jokes.explanation: Sets up the timeline for their arrangement. More functional than funny.

jokes.text: If a cow died of natural causes, I could have one of those, right? / Not if I get there first.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's ethical loophole about naturally deceased cows
jokes.punchline: Joey's competitive response about claiming the ethically-sourced meat
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Phoebe's twisted ethics and Joey's food competitiveness. Good end to their storyline.

tmdb_overview: Rachel throws a fake party for Emily to see Joshua. Pregnant Phoebe, a vegetarian, craves meat.
